title: oauth2-proxy email_domain bypass via malformed multi-@ email claims
description: isEmailValidWithDomains uses strings.HasSuffix(email, "@"+domain) to enforce the email_domain allowlist. The check accepts any email containing the substring '@<domain>' as a suffix, including malformed values with multiple '@' such as 'attacker@evil.com@company.com'. An identity provider that emits or relays such claims allows an attacker to satisfy the email_domain restriction for company.com despite belonging to evil.com. The fix rejects emails whose '@' count is not exactly one before the suffix check.
